artPrompt: The magical moment a blank rolled bill splits into two glowing duplicates and a flickering half-formed third that fades into shadow mid-conjuring, pale arcane light arcing between them above a municipal lectern. The wide frame stacks the three copies in receding depth, one cold beam isolating the foremost. Dust and gloom press in around the effect, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Two and a Half Readings of the Bill
text:
Copy target spell you control twice and a half. You may choose new targets for the copies. The half-copy does half of whatever the spell does, rounded by mutual exhaustion.
The bill is then sent back to committee, where it dies, as all bills do.
flavorText: First reading: ignored. Second reading: ignored. Half a third reading: someone fell asleep.
